Workshop.title: Workshop
Workshop.description: Represents a mechanic workshop registered in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Workshop entity.

description.description: A brief description of the workshop and its specialties.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the workshop's location.

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the workshop's location.

services.description: List of services offered by the workshop.

obdIiScannerService.description: Indicates whether the workshop offers OBDII scanner services.

ownerId.description: Reference to UserProfile representing the workshop owner. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Workshop)

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents an appointment booked by a user at a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

appointmentDateTime.description: Date and time of the appointment.

serviceType.description: Type of service requested for the appointment (e.g., general repair, OBDII scan).

notes.description: Additional notes or details for the appointment.

status.description: Status of the appointment (e.g., booked, confirmed, completed, cancelled).

ObdCode.title: ObdCode
ObdCode.description: Represents an OBDII error code and its potential causes and solutions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ObdCode entity.

description.description: A description of what the error code means.

possibleCauses.description: A list of possible causes for the error code.

commonSolutions.description: A list of common solutions to resolve the error code.

additionalNotes.description: Additional notes or relevant information about the error code.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership enables simple security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores workshop information, including ownerId for access control. No denormalized authorization data is needed as the `ownerId` is present. Enables listing all workshops.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier of the workshop.

definition.description: Stores appointment information, including userId and workshopId. This setup facilitates queries by user or workshop. Enables listing all appointments.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the appointment.

definition.description: Stores OBDII error codes and related information. Accessible by all users.

params.name: obdCodeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the OBD code.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the MechConnect application, focusing on workshop registration, appointment booking, and OBDII scanner service identification. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and query optimization.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   **Workshops:** Workshops are owned by users, and the `ownerId` is stored directly within the workshop document. This avoids the need for `get()` calls to parent user documents to verify ownership.
*   **Appointments:**  The `userId` and `workshopId` are stored directly within the appointment document. This approach provides authorization independence by avoiding the need to fetch parent documents to validate user and workshop relationships for appointment access.

**Structural Segregation and QAPs:**

*   User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`. This path-based ownership provides secure and scalable access to user data.
*   Workshops are stored in `/workshops/{workshopId}`.  Listing workshops requires no filtering. Each workshop document directly stores all the data needed for filtering.
*   Appointments are stored in `/appointments/{appointmentId}`.  To query appointments for a specific user, we would create an index on `userId`. To query for a specific workshop we would create an index on `workshopId`.
*   OBD codes are stored in `/obd_codes/{obdCodeId}`. This is a global collection.

This structure facilitates simple and robust security rules, avoiding complex hierarchical authorization dependencies and enabling efficient listing and querying of data.
